title: Head of Revenue Operations
location: Tel Aviv, Israel / New York (Globally distributed)
description: Reporting to the CRO, this hands-on leadership role builds and scales the operational backbone of Finout's GTM engine. Requires 7+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Ops, or Strategy within high-growth B2B SaaS, experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR, deep Salesforce administration and revenue analytics expertise, and cross-functional leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR. Involves collaboration with the Tel Aviv HQ across time zones. Finout is a cloud cost management company trusted by brands like The New York Times, Wiz, and Lyft.
score_reason: Exceptional match across all dimensions: 15+ years exceeds 7+ requirement, B2B SaaS expertise directly applicable, Salesforce admin strength aligns perfectly, and experience scaling GTM operations matches the hands-on Head of RevOps scope. Location is ideal with Tel Aviv HQ and global distribution model.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Finout's aggressive scaling trajectory and your RevOps leadership background immediately stood out as the operational architect this team needs. With your 15+ years building GTM engines in high-growth B2B SaaS and deep Salesforce expertise, you've likely faced the exact cross-functional complexity—Sales, Marketing, Finance alignment at scale—that Finout is solving for in cloud cost management. I'd love to explore how your experience scaling revenue operations beyond $30M ARR could accelerate Finout's next chapter.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: Accelerate revenue growth and seller productivity through data-driven investment strategy, scalable territory design, and consultative partnership with leaders. Maintains territory mapping in CRM systems (Fullcast, Salesforce), leads market expansion and capacity modeling projects. Requires 3–5+ years in Revenue Operations or Sales Strategy, deep understanding of B2B SaaS GTM models and segmentation, strong analytical skills in Excel, SQL, and BI tools (Tableau, Retool), and Salesforce proficiency. Gong is a leader in revenue intelligence.
score_reason: Exceptional match for Omri's senior RevOps expertise—territory strategy, Salesforce mastery, B2B SaaS GTM depth, and analytical rigor (Excel/SQL/BI) align perfectly. Only minor consideration: role is titled 'Manager' rather than 'Head of,' though scope and seniority requirements suggest senior-level positioning.
ai_opener: Omri here—been building revenue operations infrastructure and territory strategies across B2B SaaS for 15+ years. Your Territory Strategy & Operations role caught my attention because Gong's approach to data-driven territory design and seller productivity aligns exactly with how I've scaled GTM operations. Would be great to explore how my background in Salesforce architecture, capacity modeling, and revenue forecasting could accelerate your expansion roadmap.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Conveo is an AI research platform used by global brands like Unilever, Google, and Orange. As GTM Engineer, you'll code, ship, and iterate on internal tools and automations that make the GTM team effective — replacing manual workflows with purpose-built apps, pipelines, and AI-powered workflows. Work includes lead scoring models, attribution pipelines, onboarding flows, and AI agents that eliminate hours of manual work weekly. The company is scaling rapidly with 20x revenue growth targeted for 2026.
score_reason: GTM Engineer role is a strong conceptual fit for RevOps expertise in automation, lead scoring, and pipeline optimization, but the coding/engineering requirement is a significant mismatch for a non-technical RevOps executive. Role appears to require software engineering skills rather than RevOps strategy and operations leadership.
ai_opener: Conveo's 20x revenue growth ambition is ambitious—and I've seen firsthand how broken GTM tooling tanks that trajectory. As a RevOps leader, I've built the exact systems you're automating (lead scoring, attribution, onboarding flows), but I'm curious: are you looking for someone to architect *what* gets built, or execute the code itself? I may have a different angle on solving your GTM efficiency challenge.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Build and optimize AI outbound solutions, automated outbound sequences, and email marketing infrastructure including domain health and deliverability monitoring. Create data pipelines between marketing tools, CRM, and sales tooling for attribution and reporting, and collaborate with RevOps on lead scoring and routing. Requires 3+ years in marketing operations or GTM/growth engineering at B2B SaaS companies, hands-on experience with marketing automation tools, and strong technical skills with APIs and webhooks.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer role leveraging 15+ years B2B SaaS RevOps expertise, AI-native GTM focus, and technical proficiency with marketing automation/CRM integrations. Remote location is ideal match.
ai_opener: I've spent 15+ years optimizing go-to-market engines in B2B SaaS, and Pylon's approach to AI-native outbound automation resonates deeply—I've built similar data pipelines between marketing, CRM, and sales tools to drive attribution and lead scoring. I'd be interested in exploring how my RevOps background and technical foundation with APIs, webhooks, and marketing automation platforms could help scale your GTM infrastructure.

title: GTM Engineer
description: FacilityOS is a B2B SaaS company helping mid-to-large enterprises stay safe, compliant, and operationally sharp through its facility management platform. As GTM Engineer, you'll build, configure, and improve a collection of intelligent AI agents that help Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success work smarter. You'll work at the intersection of data, automation, and human workflows, designing how the AI systems connect, run, learn, and improve over time.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer title, AI-native systems, B2B SaaS, and RevOps automation expertise. Role requires building intelligent workflows across Sales/Marketing/CS—core RevOps competencies—though product engineering depth may be less critical than pure technical IC skills.
ai_opener: I've spent 15+ years building GTM systems that scale—from Salesforce/HubSpot infrastructure to pipeline forecasting and cross-functional automation. FacilityOS's approach to embedding AI agents directly into Sales, Marketing, and CS workflows is exactly where RevOps is headed. I'd be interested in exploring how my experience architecting data-driven go-to-market engines could accelerate your GTM intelligence platform.

title: Go-To-Market (GTM) Engineer
description: Relay is a digital banking platform for small business owners. As GTM Engineer, you'll partner with sales and marketing stakeholders to identify AI-driven solution opportunities, build and deploy AI prototypes and proofs-of-concept, integrate AI APIs, MCP Servers, and automation tools to build agents and workflows that improve the GTM motion, and proactively identify inefficiencies in sales processes by developing technical solutions focused on tool performance, adoption, and measurable efficiency gains.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer role, AI-native GTM expertise, and B2B SaaS fintech background. Remote location is ideal. Primary consideration: role emphasizes technical AI prototyping/deployment which may skew more IC/engineering-focused than traditional RevOps leadership.
ai_opener: Relay's GTM Engineer role caught my attention—building AI agents and automation workflows to improve sales motion directly aligns with how I've been architecting modern GTM stacks at B2B SaaS companies. I've spent the last few years helping teams move beyond point solutions to integrated AI-driven sales processes, especially in fintech. I'd be interested in exploring how you're thinking about the technical architecture behind your GTM infrastructure.

title: Head of Growth - SaaS B2B
description: efficiently is a fast-growing SaaS company transforming how design and construction teams collaborate. The Head of Growth owns customer acquisition, activation, and retention strategy end-to-end, defining and scaling demand generation across paid, organic, and outbound channels, and overseeing the MarTech stack (Salesforce, Klaviyo, Instantly, GA4). Requires 6–10+ years in growth marketing or demand gen, B2B SaaS experience, proven multi-channel campaign scaling tied to revenue, and strong analytical/attribution skills. Reports directly to the CEO.

However, this is primarily a growth marketing/demand gen role rather than a pure RevOps/GTM operations position—Omri's core strength is pipeline infrastructure and revenue systems, not campaign scaling and attribution modeling. The role lacks explicit RevOps, forecasting, or sales operations components.
ai_opener: Hi [Hiring Manager], I noticed efficiently is scaling design-construction collaboration in B2B SaaS—a space that demands tight revenue alignment and predictable growth infrastructure. With 15+ years building GTM engines and managing Salesforce/HubSpot stacks at scale, I've driven pipeline forecasting and demand gen attribution for high-growth SaaS teams. description: Lead marketing and growth operations analytics, connecting data to growth strategy and translating insights into operational levers with measurable revenue impact. Requires 5–7+ years in Marketing Analytics, Growth Analytics, Revenue Operations, or GTM Analytics within a SaaS or high-growth environment, deep understanding of marketing funnels, lifecycle stages, and attribution models, strong SQL skills, and experience with modern data stacks (Snowflake, dbt, BI tools).
